mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, wage growth accelerates and consumer spending expands, driving corporate revenue beats and lifting S&P 500 prices over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.5, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a shift from housing-driven growth to manufacturing-driven growth, which historically precedes margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, rising policy rates increase borrowing costs and contract credit availability, which suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in a single meeting
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered tightening episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate confirms robust aggregate demand, which directly supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%, or the rolling Spearman correlation with forward returns remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential construction financing indicates a credit crunch in the housing sector, which historically transmits to broader equity markets and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 5% over a 3-month rolling window
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a 5% RSAFS drop, or the signal shows no statistical significance in a bootstrap test with 1000 iterations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor demand expansion, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 150k month-over-month while UNRATE falls > 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of historical occurrences, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production plateaus while consumer prices accelerate, the economy enters a stagflationary regime that erodes real margins and forces equity revaluation downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0.1% while CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3%
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of stagflationary episodes, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year rolling window.
